description: OpenEye procedure-verification benchmark. 20 scene descriptions across 5 procedures, each with the correct verify_step verdict (pass/fail/uncertain).

examples.step_name: Insert M6 bolt through bracket hole into frame
examples.scene_description: Operator's right hand holds an M6 bolt vertically above the bracket. The bracket is positioned over the frame's mounting hole. The bolt has not yet entered the hole.
examples.rationale: Step is in progress but not yet completed — bolt not inserted.

examples.step_name: Insert M6 bolt through bracket hole into frame
examples.scene_description: Operator's right hand has released an M6 bolt. The bolt is fully inserted through the bracket hole into the frame, with threads engaged and the head flush against the bracket surface.
examples.rationale: Bolt visibly inserted and seated correctly.

examples.step_name: Insert M6 bolt through bracket hole into frame
examples.scene_description: Operator is inserting a bolt into the wrong hole — the secondary alignment hole, not the structural mounting hole. The mounting hole remains empty.
examples.rationale: Wrong hole — clear procedure deviation.

examples.step_name: Torque to 12 Nm with calibrated wrench
examples.scene_description: Operator uses an uncalibrated open-end wrench on the bolt. The calibrated torque wrench sits unused on the workbench.
examples.rationale: Wrong tool — uncalibrated wrench cannot satisfy torque-spec step.

examples.step_name: Wet hands with running water
examples.scene_description: Both hands are visible under running water from a tap. Hands are wet from wrists to fingertips.
examples.rationale: Both hands wet, water running — step complete.

examples.step_name: Apply antimicrobial soap
examples.scene_description: Operator pumps soap dispenser once; visible foam on right palm only. Left hand remains bare.
examples.rationale: Partial application — left hand may receive soap in next motion or may be skipped.

examples.step_name: Scrub hands for at least 20 seconds covering all surfaces
examples.scene_description: Operator briefly rubs palms together once and immediately reaches for towel. Total contact time under 2 seconds. Backs of hands and fingernails not addressed.
examples.rationale: Duration and coverage clearly insufficient.

examples.step_name: Rinse hands thoroughly under running water
examples.scene_description: Both hands held under running water for several seconds. Soap rinses visibly down the drain. Hands appear free of foam at end of frame.
examples.rationale: Thorough rinse confirmed by visible soap removal.

examples.step_name: Operate the disconnect to the OFF position
examples.scene_description: Technician's hand rotates the disconnect handle from ON to OFF. Handle is now in the down position. Indicator light on the panel goes dark.
examples.rationale: Disconnect operated correctly; indicator light confirms isolation.

examples.step_name: Apply a personal lockout device to the disconnect
examples.scene_description: Disconnect is in OFF position but no lock or tag is attached. Technician is reaching toward energized equipment.
examples.rationale: Disconnect off but not locked — technician at risk of re-energization.

examples.step_name: Apply a danger tag identifying the authorized worker
examples.scene_description: Padlock visible on disconnect lever. A tag hangs from the lock body, but the tag is blank — no worker name, no date.
examples.rationale: Tag present but illegible/unfilled — may be filled out post-frame or may indicate omission.

examples.step_name: Verify zero energy by attempting to start equipment
examples.scene_description: Technician presses the start button on the equipment. Equipment does not start. Voltage tester reads 0V on conductors.
examples.rationale: Two-method verification: start attempt fails and meter confirms 0V.

examples.step_name: Wear ANSI Z87.1 safety glasses
examples.scene_description: Operator is wearing safety glasses with visible side shields. Lenses are clear and aligned with eyes.
examples.rationale: Safety glasses with side shields present and worn correctly.

examples.step_name: Wear cut-resistant gloves rated A4 or higher
examples.scene_description: Operator wears nitrile disposable gloves. No cut-rating markings visible.
examples.rationale: Wrong glove type — nitrile disposables provide no cut resistance.

examples.step_name: Wear steel-toe boots or equivalent
examples.scene_description: Operator wears closed-toe leather boots. Toe construction is not visible from this camera angle.
examples.rationale: Footwear may meet spec but cannot be confirmed without reinforcement marking visible.

examples.step_name: Wear hearing protection rated NRR 25+
examples.scene_description: Operator wears foam earplugs with cords. Both ears visibly plugged. Operator nods affirmatively at supervisor.
examples.rationale: Hearing protection visibly in place in both ears.

examples.step_name: Visually inspect weld for porosity, undercut, and crater cracks
examples.scene_description: Inspector holds magnifier over completed weld bead. Weld surface appears uniform with no visible cracks, holes, or undercut. Inspector marks paper checklist.
examples.rationale: Inspection performed methodically; no defects called out.

examples.step_name: Visually inspect weld for porosity, undercut, and crater cracks
examples.scene_description: Multiple pinhole-sized voids visible along the weld bead. Inspector glances briefly then marks the part as accepted.
examples.rationale: Visible porosity not addressed — defect missed or ignored.

examples.step_name: Measure critical dimension with calibrated caliper
examples.scene_description: Caliper jaws contact the part at two points. Display reads 25.04 mm. Spec is 25.0 ± 0.05 mm.
examples.rationale: Measurement 25.04 mm is within tolerance band 24.95–25.05 mm.

examples.step_name: Measure critical dimension with calibrated caliper
examples.scene_description: Caliper jaws contact the part. Display reads 25.12 mm. Spec is 25.0 ± 0.05 mm. Inspector writes 25.0 on the form.
examples.rationale: Measurement out of tolerance and operator falsified result.
